summary: Part F Mobile Responsiveness Testing COMPLETE. All 21 mobile responsiveness features verified across 4 breakpoints: 320px (iPhone SE), 375px (standard mobile), 768px (tablet), 1280px (desktop). Key features working: hamburger menu, mobile sidebar slide animation, bottom nav with lg:hidden, tab-based mobile Kanban, full-screen slide-up modals, 16px input fonts (prevents iOS zoom), 44px touch targets, 2-column stat cards grid, responsive form layouts, safe area padding.

issues: Duplicate data-testid attributes for kanban columns (mobile and desktop versions both render with same testid). This is not a bug but could cause confusion in testing. Consider using unique testids like 'mobile-kanban-column-new' vs 'desktop-kanban-column-new'.

passed_tests: Login page works on mobile (320px) - form fits, submit button visible
Input fields have text-base (16px) sizing to prevent iOS zoom
Dashboard loads on mobile (375px) - welcome message, 2-col stat cards visible
Hamburger menu button visible on mobile (data-testid='mobile-menu-button')
Hamburger menu hidden on desktop (lg:hidden class working)
Mobile sidebar opens with slide animation (data-testid='mobile-sidebar')
Mobile sidebar shows nav items (Pipeline, List View, Analytics, Calendar, Portfolio)
Mobile sidebar close button functional (data-testid='close-mobile-menu')
Bottom nav visible on mobile (data-testid='mobile-bottom-nav')
Bottom nav has Dashboard/Calendar/Portfolio/Settings icons
Bottom nav hidden on desktop (lg:hidden class working)
Mobile Kanban shows tab-based column selector (data-testid='mobile-tab-new' etc)
Kanban tab navigation arrows work (data-testid='kanban-prev-column', 'kanban-next-column')
Desktop Kanban shows horizontal scrolling columns (hidden md:flex)
Add Lead modal: full-screen slide-up on mobile (width=375px, height=771px)
Add Lead modal: centered on desktop (max-w-2xl)
Add Lead form: single column on mobile (grid-cols-1)
Lead Detail modal: full-screen slide-up on mobile
Lead Detail tabs: all 5 tabs accessible (Activity, Quotes, Files, Details, Deliverables)
Mobile filter toggle button with badge count (data-testid='mobile-filter-toggle')
Touch targets: Logout=44px, Hamburger=44px, Add Lead=44px, Bottom nav=49px (all >= 44px)
Stat cards in 2-column grid on mobile (same row, ~166px each)
List view table responsive with overflow-x-auto and min-w-[500px]
Search input responsive width, accessible on mobile
Safe area padding on bottom nav (safe-bottom class)
Input font-size 16px enforced via CSS !important rule
critical_code_review_comments: MobileBottomNav: Properly implements lg:hidden and safe-bottom classes
KanbanBoard: Correct use of md:hidden for mobile tabs and hidden md:flex for desktop columns
Dashboard: Hamburger has lg:hidden touch-target class, mobile sidebar is properly animated
AddLeadModal: Uses grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 for responsive form layout
index.css: Enforces 16px font on inputs via !important rule to prevent iOS zoom
index.css: touch-target class properly sets min-height: 44px and min-width: 44px
index.css: safe-bottom uses env(safe-area-inset-bottom) for notched devices
tailwind.config.js: slide-up-full animation uses cubic-bezier for smooth slide-up effect
seed_data_creation: None - used existing leads from previous phases
context_for_next_testing_agent: Part F Mobile Responsiveness fully tested. All breakpoints verified: 320px (iPhone SE), 375px (standard mobile), 768px (tablet), 1280px (desktop). Key data-testids for mobile features: mobile-menu-button, mobile-sidebar, close-mobile-menu, mobile-bottom-nav, bottom-nav-kanban/calendar/portfolio/settings, mobile-tab-new/contacted/quoted/negotiating/booked, kanban-prev-column, kanban-next-column, mobile-filter-toggle. CSS classes verified: lg:hidden, touch-target, safe-bottom. Input font-size 16px prevents iOS zoom.

responsive_features_verified.hamburger_menu: Visible on mobile (lg:hidden), hidden on desktop
responsive_features_verified.mobile_kanban: Tab-based column selector with navigation arrows
responsive_features_verified.modals: Full-screen slide-up on mobile, centered on desktop
responsive_features_verified.form_layouts: grid-cols-1 on mobile, md:grid-cols-2 on desktop
